refnamediv.refpurpose: apply a function to each column of the table/timeseries

refsynopsisdiv.title: Syntax
refsynopsisdiv.synopsis: v = varfun(f, t) v = varfun(f, t, OptionName, Value, ...)

listitem.para: function to apply with prototype: y = f(x)
f must take one input argument
table or timeseries object in input
variables values are used to create unique groups.
the function will be apply only on this specified variables.
output argument - table or timeseries object (same object as t).

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: The varfun function returns a table or timeseries (same object as t) where each variable (columns) contains the result of the function applied to each variable of t.
v = varfun(f, t) applies the function f to each variable of t.

To apply the function only on certain table variables, InputVariables must be specified: v = varfun(f, t, "InputVariables", ["Var1", ...,]). It is also possible to use the syntax v = varfun(f, t(:, ["Var1", ...,])).

v = varfun(f, t, "GroupingVariables", ["Var1", ...,]) allows to specify the variables used to group the table or timeseries. The function f will be applied to each group in each variable of t. The expected result of function f must be a scalar.
